raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a follow-up complaint regarding Citibanks handling of two unauthorized transactions on my account : XXXX XXXXXXXX ( {$2500.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, dated XX/XX/year> ) XXXX XXXXXXXX ( {$1000.00} XXXX XXXX, dated XX/XX/year> ) In my prior complaint, I requested that Citibank properly investigate these transactions as unauthorized ( card-not-present fraud ). Citibank has now responded and acknowledged that the transactions are being treated as fraudulent, stating that the account will be security closed and the charges flagged as fraud.

However, despite acknowledging the transactions as fraudulent, Citibank has refused to issue any reimbursement or credit for these charges.

This creates a clear inconsistency. If the transactions are recognized as unauthorized, it is unclear why I am being held financially responsible. Citibank has not provided a clear explanation of how liability was determined or why reimbursement is being denied despite classifying the transactions as fraud.

Additionally, the transactions were completed using a stored payment method without step-up authentication such as CVV re-entry, one-time passcode, or 3D Secure verification. There is no evidence demonstrating that I, or any authorized user on the account, participated in or authorized these transactions at the time they occurred.

Citibanks response does not address the substance of the unauthorized use, nor does it explain how their determination aligns with consumer protection expectations for unauthorized transactions.
